It is bad like really bad

developer_response.body: Sure, it's different to Gorilla Tag, but does that make it bad? We have spent a lot of time trying to design controls that work with your head to control direction and two buttons: one to move forward and one to bite. Part of the challenge of being a shark is having no hands and only being able to use your mouth!

reviews.review_description: The game sharks is a really fun game and I like it because we can kill each other and eat fish and crabs and I really like the movement and it's realistic how we bleed if we get bit by other sharks in conclusion this game is really fun I hope you see this
Www

reviews.review_title: I like the game but i have few ideas
reviews.review_description: The game overall is super fun and a very cool idea but there are a few things i think need tweaking, like the selling system. I can kinda understand why it's required to have two people in order to scrap stuff but the problem with that is either people will take the gold fish you get and leave you nothin everytime or people will never join your server and it becomes physically impossible to sufficiently earn gold fish especially if you're a late night player. Another problem I've come into is that there's no way to get the diver back once someone sends him up which means if you get the quest to hold him for 60 seconds it becomes impossible to do, so it would be nice to have a way to get him back some how. Overall i really do love this game and am excited to see what direction it takes in the future!

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing and sending through these ideas - and for the review. We always wanted the salvage to be a bit of a social experience where you need to negotiate with other players to work out a fair way of playing, or make or invite friends you can trust to play in a private room. With the diver, if you keep filling the salvage cage more divers will spawn to replace the salvaged items, or you can all leave the room and come back in to reset the original items. We are also working on new game modes to help you earn more goldfish. I've also sent you a big pack of goldfish, so check your messages in the headset!

reviews.review_description: Good

This game is awesome. I hope it never dies out but the only thing is kids are saying bad words in it and also kids are saying the N-word a lot but other than that everything is great. The graphics is great. Everything is like beautiful because I'm Mr_￼Sharky on YouTube and I love this.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! Improving player behavior is a top priority for us, we are adding more moderators everyday and working to improve the voice chat. In public rooms you can access the 'Mute' menu to instantly silence particular players: this preference will persist even if you are in the same game in the future (you can also unmute players if you want to give them another chance). You can also report players via the Quest menu.  Or the best solution is to create a private room and invite your friends!

developer_response.body: Thanks - see you in there!

reviews.review_description: I genuinely didn't know there was only two people working on this game. I'm sorry if I asked for a lot. The game is genuinely very fun and I was just wondering if that would be a possibility. Amazing game tho

developer_response.body: No problem! 🤘 Hey, thanks for playing and the feedback! There are tons of ways to change the look of your shark, have you tried any of the cosmetics? e.g. if you want a tiger sharky-looking skin here's an in-game code to unlock: "SPOTTY". It would be cool to have different shark models, but that would mean we'd have to create custom versions of all the cosmetics for each of the models - which is too much work right now (there are only two of us making this game). Hopefully if we can get more people playing and buying premium skins we can add more resources to create more playable models. Maybe we can do a 'Hammerhead' cosmetic that could be added to the existing shark model?

developer_response.body: Glad we could help! Thanks for your patience: our game is a long way from being perfect, but the two of us are working hard to make it the best game we can. If anyone else experiences any issues or bugs, please visit our discord (ezone.com/discord) and we'll get you sorted out. Thanks for playing!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback about the camera view: a few players have commented that they would prefer first person. I think it makes sense for a game where you have two hands - like in Gorilla Tag - where you can hold up your hands and see what you a carrying/holding - but for a shark where you use your mouth to grab and carry things all you would see in first person is the object floating in front of you. Anyway, we'd love to see you come back and try it again to see what you think!

reviews.review_title: Good concept brakes a rule of VR
reviews.review_description: Grate concept and could be a good game. However, the 3rd person in VR does not work. Also, moving your head to move the sharks head wall that far back makes it harder to control, where if you used the sticks, it could fix this. Grate concept not great execution. The mandatory 3rd person makes this 2 star for me.

developer_response.body: Hey, thanks for giving it a try anyway - too bad the 3rd person view didn't gel for you. We've had a lot of positive feedback from players who really like being able to see the shark swimming around, so hopefully potential players reading your review will be intrigued enough to download it for free and judge for themselves. Cheers!

reviews.review_description: Game doesn't support meta quest 2 pro controllers, looks super fun though.

developer_response.body: Thanks for letting us know. We have fixed the issue with the pro controllers in the latest update - check it out and let us know!

reviews.review_description: I loved the game hungry shark. I thought that this would be similar. I was bummed when I found that I couldn’t eat the scuba diver.  The zombie shark game. I don’t think there’s anyway you can win you can’t see when they’re coming up behind you.  It’s a bloodbath and it’s over quickly.  I look forward to seeing what else they roll out. I would like to see the game be similar to hungry shark I could really rip through the water, take leaps out to eat people on shore and go down into the caves   Aah good times!

developer_response.body: Even though you can't eat the diver, sharks are still finding ways to torment the poor guy! Hopefully you'll give the 'zombie' mode another try: there are a few different ways to play and good use of the environment and power-ups (especially invisibility) can really help. Maybe see you in there!

It’s really fun game and really interesting. Hopefully there’s gonna be new sharks and there’s multiplayer in the best part it is it’s free. There’s only one thing I wish they had was to change the view of your camera. If there is there I don’t know

developer_response.body: We are adding new outfits and skins with every update so you should be able to find a look that suits your shark. A few players have commented that they would prefer a first person view but because you are using your mouth to grab and carry things all you would see in first person is the object floating in front of you - and you wouldn't be able to see what your shark looks like while you're playing!

reviews.review_description:
novel and intuitive movement, good graphics, gameplay not very exciting (gets boring after 10 minutes), no voice chat? (I don't hear any chat except prerecorded jokes, which were pretty good), still not many players but just enough during the rush hours.

I think the main problem is the locomotion doesn't have much room for skill growth, and it's also too monotonous (one speed). Therefore it gets boring quick.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback JimZM! We decided to let players use the built-in Quest friends chat. We are looking into adding proximity voice chat if there is enough interest from players. If you've got some ideas on how we can improve the skill growth we'd love to chat - drop in to our discord (https://ezone.com/discord) and we'll see what we can do!

reviews.review_description: I enjoy the game a lot it's fun and helps me with fear of the ocean and sharks  🦈

I really like the shark physics and swimming around. And the cross-play with flat screen PC players is awesome. And the last patch really improved things. But no one is online ever. I gathered some friends to try the online part, the zombie tag is fun for a quick party game. The full game needs more modes, or bots, or single player content. I'd really love to swim with my shark in a simple story mode. Or playing some PVE mode with friends for example put a large school of fish to the map and let's see who can catch more. Or hunt divers who can attack back with a harpoon gun. Or make an underwater race track and make it into a swimming Mario Kart, you already have crazy power ups.
Now all we need is voice chat, privet lobbies, a casual mode, more maps, more cosmetics, and a campaign.

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing! More modes, maps and cosmetics on the way.

reviews.review_description: not much but it is still in early access has great potential and could possibly create a new genre of vr games

developer_response.body: We've got big plans! Thanks for being an early player and being part of the evolution of the game. If you've got suggestions, please come chat with us in our discord - https://ezone.com/discord - cheers!

reviews.review_title: great but i have one request
reviews.review_description: I want a singleplayer story mode for this, it would be awesome.

developer_response.body: ....we'll see what we can do!
